## Problem

The promotions section served by `sv-playbook serve` renders mojibake: `content/ui/index.html` contains 10 double-encoded UTF-8 sequences (`Ã¡`, `Ã³`, `Ãš`) introduced when the promotion projection UI was authored. The console shows "Estado mecÃ¡nico", "integraciÃ³n", "Ãšltimo cambio" instead of "mecánico", "integración", "Último cambio". No gate inspects static UI asset encoding, so corrupted text can reach the human surface silently (HJ-015: UI is a product surface; ENTRY-004: no claim without evidence).

## Task

1. Add `src/serve/ui-static-assets.test.ts` with a test named exactly `static UI assets contain no double-encoded UTF-8 sequences` that scans every `content/ui/**/*.{html,js,css}` file and fails when it finds the double-encoding signature (`Ã` followed by a second mojibake byte pattern, i.e. U+00C3 followed by a character in the Latin-1 Supplement range produced by re-encoding UTF-8 as Latin-1). The test must pass on clean assets and a companion case must prove the detector fails on a corrupted fixture string.
2. Fix the 10 sequences in `content/ui/index.html`: `Ã¡`→`á`, `Ã³`→`ó`, `Ãš`→`Ú`. No other content changes.
3. Record the incident in `docs/backlog.md` as a graduated entry pointing to this packet.

## RED test

- Test: `static UI assets contain no double-encoded UTF-8 sequences` in `src/serve/ui-static-assets.test.ts`.
- Expected failure cause: the test name itself (node:test prints the failing test name); it fails today because `content/ui/index.html` contains the sequences.

## Reuse pointers

- `src/serve/server.ts` serves these assets from `content/ui` (`UI_ROOT`).
- `src/check/source-tree.ts` shows how the project walks source trees for mechanical checks.

## Stop conditions

- Mojibake appears in any file other than `content/ui/index.html` → stop, re-scope.
- The fix requires editing any file outside the write_set → stop, re-scope.
- The detector test cannot distinguish clean text from double-encoded text without false positives on legitimate `Ã` usage → stop and report the counterexample.

## Review instructions

The verdict JSON MUST use this exact envelope (the promotion controller validates it mechanically and rejects anything else):

```json
{
  "kind": "review-verdict",
  "payload": {
    "verdict": "APPROVED | REQUEST CHANGES",
    "candidateSha": "<full candidate sha under review>",
    "workDefinitionRef": { "id": "<work definition id>", "version": <integer>, "digest": "<sha256:...>" }
  }
}
```

Copy `workDefinitionRef` verbatim from the review candidate artifact's `workDefinition` field (id, version, digest) — do NOT substitute a bare `packetId` field. Extra payload fields (`summary`, `findings`, `evidenceReferences`, `residualRisk`) are welcome.

- RED run of `node --test dist/serve/ui-static-assets.test.js` failing with the registered expected cause, recorded as a task note before the fix.
- `npm run verify` green after the fix (typecheck, lint, test, check).
- Final SHA and independent review run id (evidence_required: `final-sha`, `independent-review`). First review run `RUN-019f66c6-c760-7498-b858-6375db17cfbe` failed with `INVALID_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` (terminal, no verdict produced); second run `RUN-019f66d3-91cf-775e-b835-7e9dc666ac93` returned APPROVED but the promotion controller rejected the verdict envelope (missing `payload.workDefinitionRef`), so v4 adds the explicit contract under Review instructions.
- Promotion receipt produced by `promotion run` closing the task.
